caption.text: here's what I no longer want to see:
empty dashboards displaying "no data yet" with no call-to-action.
It doesn’t guide—it abandons the user from the very first second.
"Create" buttons without specifying what. Create a project,
If the action isn’t clear, neither is the intent.
onboarding with 10 to 12 form fields all at once.
Until the value is perceived, every field becomes a friction point.
interfaces where everything is at the same level. If everything is important,
the user has absolutely no idea where to start.
And finally, journeys designed to be exhaustive instead of progressive.
A good product doesn’t show everything—it reveals at the right moment.
In 2026, I want to see fewer explanations,
And of course, I wish you a wonderful year in 2026!
